comment_text.ud2.7:5.1: The PTS reading piyarūpāsāta is tempting, and supported by the Udānavarga’s priyarūpasātagrathitā. The commentary reads sukhavedanassādena, which appears to support assāda. But perhaps originally sāta was glossed with sukhavedanā, and assāda was read back into the text by mistake.

root_text.ud2.7:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud2.7:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa upāsakassa ekaputtako piyo manāpo kālaṅkato hoti.
root_text.ud2.7:2.1: Atha kho sambahulā upāsakā allavatthā allakesā divā divassa yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinne kho te upāsake bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.7:2.2: “kiṁ nu kho tumhe, upāsakā, allavatthā allakesā idhūpasaṅkamantā divā divassā”ti?
root_text.ud2.7:3.1: Evaṁ vutte, so upāsako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.7:3.2: “mayhaṁ kho, bhante, ekaputtako piyo manāpo kālaṅkato.
root_text.ud2.7:3.3: Tena mayaṁ allavatthā allakesā idhūpasaṅkamantā divā divassā”ti.
root_text.ud2.7:4.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud2.7:6.1: Ye ve divā ca ratto ca,

translation_text.ud2.7:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud2.7:1.3: Now at that time a certain lay follower’s dear and beloved only child passed away.
translation_text.ud2.7:2.1: Then in the middle of the day several lay followers with wet clothes and hair went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to them:
translation_text.ud2.7:2.2: “Why, lay followers, have you come here in the middle of the day with wet clothes and hair?”
translation_text.ud2.7:3.2: “Sir, my dear and beloved only child has passed away.
translation_text.ud2.7:3.3: That’s why we came here in the middle of the day with wet clothes and hair.”
translation_text.ud2.7:4.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud2.7:5.1: “Hosts of gods and most human beings are bound
translation_text.ud2.7:5.2: to what seems dear and pleasant.
translation_text.ud2.7:5.4: they fall under the sway of the King of Death.
translation_text.ud2.7:6.1: The diligent, who day and night
translation_text.ud2.7:6.3: dig out the root of gloom—
translation_text.ud2.7:6.4: Death’s bait so hard to escape.”

reference_text.ud2.7:5.1: dr25.125, mc0.112, ms18Ud_113, pts-vp-pli15, vns20, vri47.85
